items.title: E-motorcyclist filmed doing wheelie, crashing while police attend another collision

children.content: SA Police publishes a video of a teenager on an electric motorcycle doing a wheelie and then crashing into a car while they were attending another collision at the same location in Adelaide's southern suburbs.

image.altText: Dashcam footage of crash site.

gallery.altText: A motorcycle rider doing a wheelie past commercial buildings

thumbnail.altText: A motorcycle rider doing a wheelie past commercial buildings

items.title: More rain to follow overnight storm and 50k lightning strikes in parts of SA

children.content: Wet and wild weather is lashing parts of South Australia with the Bureau of Meteorology warning about wind risk during storms.

image.altText: People cross a pedestrian crossing while carrying umbrellas with a wet ground

items.title: Woman 'still here to see grandkids grow' welcomes free HIV treatment in SA

children.content: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

image.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

items.title: Volunteers say National Trust of SA warned them against speaking publicly

children.content: Volunteers who spoke to the media say they felt "threatened" with formal warnings from the National Trust of South Australia. They are now preparing to share their experiences in a parliamentary inquiry.

gallery.altText: A woman with cropped white hair and a necklace in a living room

thumbnail.altText: A woman with cropped white hair and a necklace in a living room

items.title: Patrons farewell historical pub in outback town amid spate of closures

children.content: There used to be more than 70 pubs in Broken Hill, but now only a handful remain. The Australian Hotels Association warns hotels in remote locations need to diversify their business model in order to survive a rapidly changing hospitality landscape.

image.altText: An older man standing outside a country pub with a grimace on his face, as it rains in the background

items.title: 'One last crack' to restart Whyalla’s blast furnace as workers face nervous wait

children.content: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

image.altText: A man wearing an orange jacket and white hard hat stands outside a large industrial building

items.title: 'No genuine opportunity' to prevent crash that killed two women, coroner finds

children.content: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

image.altText: An image of Joanne Shanahan and Tania McNeill.

items.title: Thunderbirds co-captain to lead 'connected' team in grand final repeat

children.content: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are looking to win their third premiership in four years, with co-captain Georgie Horjus putting their success down to strong team connections on and off the court.

image.altText: Three women playing netball in a stadium

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast

children.content: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

image.altText: A bunch of flowers placed next to a road.

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia

children.content: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

image.altText: A British midwife with brown hair stands next to her husband in front of their home in Whyalla

items.title: Rex Airlines breached continuous disclosure obligations

children.content: Rex contravened its continuous disclosure obligations, misleading the market, the NSW Supreme Court found on Tuesday.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers

children.content: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

image.altText: two large cuttlefish near rocks underwater

images.altText: Mexico players join in a massive huddle in celebration of a goal
Two Mexican fans wearing wet-weather ponchos hug in the stands
France players converge to celebrate a Bradley Barcola goal

articles.title: 'No tomorrow' mindset for Socceroos ahead of World Cup clash with Egypt

children.content: The Socceroos' World Cup adventure will either end or become historic when they face Egypt on Friday.

image.altText: Jordan Bos of Australia steals the ball from Julio Enciso of Paraguay

articles.title: French unity on display as their ruthless run through World Cup continues

children.content: In a French team littered with superstars it is something intangible that could stop them taking another World Cup title, following another clinical display against Sweden in the round of 32.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappe holds his arms in the air in celebration

children.content: Mexico makes a brilliant start against Ecuador at the Azteca, after Kylian Mbappé scores twice for France and Erling Haaland nets an 88th-minute winner for Norway. Follow live.

items.title: Mexico on fire as Azteca erupts with incredible atmosphere

updates.title: FT: Mexico books place in Round of 16 after two first-half goals
HT: Mexico takes commanding lead over Ecuador with two first-half goals

items.title: PM sidesteps question on whether house prices will continue falling

children.content: Anthony Albanese has sidestepped questions on whether house prices will continue falling, following the release of data today showing a 0.4 per cent drop in home values. Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese raises his hand while speaking in parliament.

items.title: Liberals can't keep on track even with a prize in sight

children.content: The Liberals, it seems, can't resist returning to damaging infighting, even in the one part of the country where things have been going quite encouragingly for them.

image.altText: Moira Deeming appears serious as she walks through the parliament carpark, wearing a purple blazer.

items.title: Campers hide in tents as suspected foreign boat arrives at remote Qld tourist beach

children.content: The group allegedly came ashore from a boat at Pennefather Beach in the early hours of yesterday Tuesday morning.

gallery.altText: A helicopter on a coastline with four men dressed in black.

thumbnail.altText: A helicopter on a coastline with four men dressed in black.

items.title: Vanuatu deal is significant but a harbinger of what's to come in the Pacific

children.content: Although a big moment in diplomacy, the Vanuatu agreement has come at a clear reputational cost to Australia.

image.altText: Jotham Napat and Anthony Albanese hold up signed documents.

items.title: ACCC blocks Coles supermarket to protect regional city's independents

children.content: In an unprecedented move, Australia's consumer watchdog has exercised new powers to block the opening of a Coles supermarket, on the grounds it could knock out smaller competitors in the surrounding region.

image.altText: The owner of a supermarket standing in fruit and vegetable isle with arms crossed.

items.title: Why Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams before their match

children.content: Australian Maya Joint had to catch a glimpse of Serena Williams in the days leading up to their Wimbledon opener just to remove some of the mythos around the 23-time grand slam champion.

articles.title: Who is Maya Joint, Australian conqueror of Serena at Wimbledon?

children.content: Maya Joint was born in April 2006 when Serena Williams had already won seven Grand Slam singles titles. So who is the 20-year-old who just defeated a tennis legend in her return to the All England Club?

image.altText: A red-headed female tennis player in white pokes tongue out on Wimbledon centre court

articles.title: Australian rising star takes down Serena Williams in Wimbledon return

children.content: Australian Maya Joint holds her nerve to beat tennis legend Serena Williams in the 23-time grand slam champion's first professional tennis match in four years.

image.altText: Maya Joint shakes Serena Williams's hand over the net at Wimbledon.

gallery.altText: Serena Williams and Maya Joint walk onto centre court at Wimbledon.
Maya Joint clenches her fist and shouts during a match at Wimbledon.
Maya Joint speaks on centre court at Wimbledon.

thumbnail.altText: Serena Williams and Maya Joint walk onto centre court at Wimbledon.
Maya Joint clenches her fist and shouts during a match at Wimbledon.
Maya Joint speaks on centre court at Wimbledon.

items.title: Boag's beer brand not for sale, but owner open to offers

children.content: The union representing Boag's brewery workers in Launceston wants its owner, Lion Australia, to sell the brand along with the brewery. But the company's response has left workers confused.

image.altText: A red brick building with a large Boags Brewery sign.

items.title: Mother of murdered Toyah Cordingley gives rare interview to thank police officers

children.content: Eight years on from her daughter's brutal murder, Vanessa Gardiner says nominating the investigating offices for an award was one of the few ways she could express her gratitude.

image.altText: Woman wearing head scarf beside woman with glasses with her hand on her shoulder

items.title: Country-of-origin labelling laws set to rewrite seafood menus

image.altText: image of imported and australian fish on display in shop

children.content: New labelling laws for hospitality venues serving seafood should help diners know exactly what they are eating.

items.title: Do you have what it takes to best our travel quiz?

children.content: Where would you find the iconic Forrest Gump bench? And do you know where this bridge is?

image.altText: The sun sets behind Brisbane's Story Bridge and Brisbane River in the CBD.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

image.altText: A man stands in a stairwell

items.title: New financial year, new rules and how today's changes affect your wallet

children.content: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

items.title: ASX dips, while AI-buying frenzy drives Dow to record high

children.content: The Australian share market eases slightly, even as an AI stock-led rally boosted the Dow Jones to a record high for a second straight day. Follow live.

image.altText: ASX logo and board

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: Plan to add hundreds of parking meters at Sydney beaches rejected

children.content: Randwick councillors vote against a plan to introduce hundreds of parking meters at some of Sydney's popular eastern beaches, with council staff to now look for other ways to improve the turnover of parking spaces.

image.altText: beachgoers on the sand at a beach

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

image.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo

items.title: BHP shows off two electric trucks — with 62 diesel ones on order

children.content: BHP and Rio Tinto have put their two battery-electric trucks on show, but is an aversion to risk stifling meaningful change?

gallery.altText: A huge, yellow mining dump truck on a red dirt mine site.

thumbnail.altText: A huge, yellow mining dump truck on a red dirt mine site.

items.title: Childcare provider stripped of funding labels decision 'devastating'

children.content: A Victorian childcare provider stripped of its federal government funding over unresolved safety breaches has vowed to challenge the decision.

image.altText: A non-identifiable child playing with toys.

items.title: Coalition avoids direct One Nation messaging in daily talking points

children.content: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

image.altText: A man in a suit takes questions from journalists
